sourceMovie.title: Titanic
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Titanic (1997) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. James Cameron's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Twilight
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Titanic clicked for you, Twilight (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Titanic?
faq.answer: Try Twilight, The Curious Case of Benjamin Button, The Notebook—each shares DNA with Titanic while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Titanic?
faq.answer: Titanic (1997) is best described as Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Titanic worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
